ev.headline: Toyota Prius Outselling Every Battery-Electric Vehicle In America but Two
ev.summary: Despite EV industry growth expectations, the 2026 Toyota Prius is outselling nearly every battery-electric vehicle in the U.S. market as consumers increasingly favor hybrids over pure electric options.

ev.headline: Lucid Motors Cuts 12% of U.S. Salaried Workforce
ev.summary: Lucid Motors announced layoffs affecting 12% of its U.S. salaried workforce across multiple states and facilities, reflecting ongoing challenges in the EV startup sector.

ev.headline: Nio Sets New Battery Swap Record for Fourth Consecutive Day
ev.summary: Chinese EV maker Nio completed 175,976 battery swap services on February 21, achieving its fourth consecutive daily record and demonstrating the viability of battery-as-a-service infrastructure.

ev.headline: Tesla Cybertruck Dual-Motor AWD Model Arrives at $59,990, Elon Musk Signals Limited Availability
ev.summary: Tesla launched a new dual-motor all-wheel-drive Cybertruck starting at $59,990, but CEO Elon Musk indicated the model will only be available for the next 10 days, creating urgency for buyers.

ev.headline: Court Rules Trump Administration Unlawfully Suspended EV Infrastructure Funding
ev.summary: A federal judge blocked the Trump administration's suspension of the $5 billion National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure program, allowing states to access funds for charging infrastructure expansion.

ev.headline: Automakers Face Over $50 Billion in EV Strategy Writedowns Amid Market Slowdown
ev.summary: Major automakers including Stellantis ($26B), Ford ($19.5B), GM ($6B), and Volkswagen ($6B) have announced massive EV-related writedowns as they pivot away from aggressive electrification targets.

ev.headline: BYD Super-e Platform Delivers 400km Range in 5 Minutes with 1MW Charging
ev.summary: BYD's new Super-e platform achieved a charging benchmark of approximately 400 kilometers of range in just 5 minutes using next-generation silicon carbide chips and 1000V architecture.

ev.headline: Rivian Achieves First Full-Year Gross Profit with $144 Million in 2025
ev.summary: Rivian announced its first annual gross profit of $144 million, representing a $1.3 billion improvement, as the company completes R2 manufacturing validation ahead of Q2 2026 deliveries.

v2x.headline: Hyundai Motor Group Accelerates V2X Rollout with V2H and V2G Services
v2x.summary: Hyundai Motor Group is expanding Vehicle-to-Everything services worldwide, launching V2H services in the US starting with IONIQ 9 and EV6 models, while offering V2G in Korea and Europe.

v2x.headline: GM Energy Pursues Vehicle-to-Grid Certification with Positive Testing Results
v2x.summary: General Motors reports positive internal testing of V2G capability and is working to certify it with local utilities, moving closer to grid integration. The company sold over 246,000 V2H-capable vehicles in 2025.

v2x.headline: Massachusetts Deploys Free Bidirectional EV Chargers in First-of-Its-Kind V2X Program
v2x.summary: Massachusetts is installing 100 free bidirectional V2X chargers across schools, municipalities and residents, expected to add 1.5 MW of distributed storage by summer 2026 with comprehensive V2X guidebook publication.

v2x.headline: V2H Charging Could Save EV Owners Thousands and Slash Emissions Study Finds
v2x.summary: First large-scale national study shows V2H charging can save owners $2,400-$5,600 over vehicle lifetime and reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 70-250%, potentially making EVs net-negative on household carbon.

v2x.headline: UK V2G Market Faces Challenges with CCS Testing Failures, But Progress Continues
v2x.summary: UK's CCS-based V2G remains in development with 11 of 14 type-testing applications failing, though Nissan's AC-based approach gained official grid certification after University of Nottingham trial.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G Integration
v2x.summary: Enphase Energy introduced its IQ Bidirectional EV Charger architecture for home backup and grid services, designed to be cost-effective and compatible with existing home electrical systems.

v2x.headline: Honda Announces Bidirectional V2X Capability for Upcoming Acura RSX
v2x.summary: Honda announced its upcoming Acura RSX will feature bidirectional home charging, previewing future support for V2H and V2G across Honda's global EV platform.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging Technology Reaches Mainstream Adoption in 2026
v2x.summary: Bidirectional charging is achieving mainstream adoption in 2026 with growing manufacturer support, new economic incentives from utilities, and established models like Ford F-150 Lightning providing full V2X capabilities.
